title: aldama rubra
intro: Aldama rubra, also known as the red alder, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Aldama rubra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe coughs and colds.
